Platelets [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count
Platelet distribution width [Entitic volume] in Blood by Automated count
Platelet [Entitic mean volume] in Blood by Automated count
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The results show elevated HbA1c (6.3%) indicating prediabetes or early diabetes, with low fasting glucose possibly due to dietary factors or medication. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated, increasing cardiovascular risk. Chloride is slightly high, but other electrolytes and renal function are normal. Hemoglobin is mildly elevated, which may warrant further evaluation. Platelet distribution width is high, but platelet count is normal. Overall, monitor glycemic control and lipid profile; consider further assessment for elevated hemoglobin.

text.div: Provider Organization: Facharzt für Allgemeinmedizin Johann Holbach
